title: Credee
subtitle: Production FinTech Platform
summary: Stripe Connect-powered B2B payment financing engine — installment plans, OBO payouts, Terminal S700, and dynamic fees backed by async workers and durable webhook-driven state.
role: Senior Full Stack Developer — financing engine, payment infrastructure, and worker architecture.
architecture: Client actions enter Node APIs that validate and authorize every money-adjacent mutation.
Stripe Connect webhooks and fee calculations run through idempotent BullMQ jobs.
Redis protects hot reads while PostgreSQL and MySQL remain the source of truth for financial state.
lessons: Payment state should be event-driven, not click-optimistic.
Dynamic fees need auditable rules and safe retries when providers disagree.
Installment financing workflows are underwriting logic first, payment plumbing second.

title: Practina
subtitle: Production AI Marketing Platform
summary: AI content generation, sentiment analysis, Meta/Google Ads automation, and media pipelines built for reliability under real campaign load.
role: Full Stack Developer — AI integrations, async pipelines, and platform reliability.
architecture: Generation and publishing are separated so prompts never block social delivery.
BullMQ owns retries, rate limits, and provider outages across OpenAI, Meta Ads, and Google Ads APIs.
Redis coordinates short-lived job and schedule state for bursty campaign activity.
lessons: AI features need queues, cost controls, and explicit failure modes from day one.
Sentiment analysis and send-time decisioning are systems problems — not only prompt tweaks.
Provider APIs fail; product flows should degrade without losing user intent.

title: AI Interview Copilot
subtitle: Personal Project
summary: AI-powered interview preparation assistant that generates contextual interview responses and explanations using LLMs.
role: Solo builder — product, API, and LLM interaction design.
architecture: Thin Next.js client over a typed Node API for prompt construction and response shaping.
OpenAI calls stay behind explicit request boundaries for faster iteration and safer defaults.
lessons: Good interview output depends more on context packaging than model novelty.
Explanations matter as much as answers when the product is about learning.

title: AI Credit Scoring Assistant
subtitle: Personal Project
summary: AI-powered assistant for borrower analysis, credit evaluation, and explainable financial decision support.
role: Solo builder — reasoning UX, evaluation flow, and explainability.
architecture: Structured borrower inputs feed an evaluation pipeline that returns decisions with rationale.
Model outputs are shaped for auditability instead of free-form persuasion.
lessons: In credit-adjacent systems, explanation quality is part of the product.
Structured inputs beat long unstructured chats when judgments must be compared.
